response.justification: Bogdan Szabo brings 15+ years of broad software engineering experience, strong TypeScript skills, and recent hands-on work with MCP (Model Context Protocol) tooling and agentic workflow infrastructure in his current role. However, the position requires a minimum of 8 years building AI-driven solutions, and his AI-specific experience is limited to approximately one year. Critical gaps include no mention of Python (the dominant language for AI/ML engineering), no evidence of vector database or RAG architecture knowledge, no documented use of multi-agent frameworks such as LangGraph or CrewAI, and no LLM optimization expertise. His career is primarily grounded in web, embedded, and data-pipeline engineering rather than applied GenAI, making him materially underqualified for a Staff-level forward-deployed AI engineering role.
strengths: Extensive full-stack and systems engineering background (15+ years) with TypeScript, Node.js, high-throughput microservices, and cloud infrastructure (AWS CDK)
Active involvement in MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks in current role at re:cinq, demonstrating genuine transition toward AI engineering
Demonstrated ability to build and maintain real-world data platforms (event-driven geo-data platform, high-volume data integration at Sociomantic handling millions of requests/second)
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the resume — a critical omission for a role where Python is the primary AI/ML development language
No evidence of vector database usage, RAG architectures, LLM performance evaluation, or prompt optimization — all of which are required or strongly preferred qualifications
AI-specific experience appears limited to the current role (April 2025–present, roughly one year), far below the 8-year minimum the role requires for production-grade AI solution delivery

key_factors.factor: AI experience depth (8yr req vs ~1yr actual)
Absent Python and AI/ML toolchain knowledge
